material_type: Cold-formed ferritic stainless steel (EN 1.4003 and EN 1.4509)
judgement_calls: Some specimens failed by combined local and overall buckling so interactive failures need to be reviewed

study_id_notes: 1 experimental batch (behzadi2021_A) comprising all 5 physical column tests. They share the exact same hot-rolled 70×70×10 cross-section profile and physical coupon test results
judgement_calls: FEA rows excluded at this stage, Derived Le using k-0.5. This study uses equal-angle sections which introudces asymmetric cross-section nature --> may not be relevant in later stages

study_id_notes: 4 batches cleanly separated by the four tested cross-section sizes (theofanous2009_A through D). theofanous2009_A contained only stub columns and was fully excluded
judgement_calls: Prioritised compressive flat material properties over tensile for extraction. Excluded all 8 stub columns because they failed by pure local buckling. FEA excluded for now, raw w0 was recorded however, a combined loading e was enforced to ensure minimum total effective imperfection was L/1500.

study_id_notes: 6 distinct batches (huang2013_A through F) corresponding to the 6 tested cross-section profiles. All specimens in huang_F compeltely excluded due to failure_mode
judgement_calls: n_source = derived, 22 specimens included due to yielding, interactive mode or local buckling failure, extracted material properties from tensile flat coupons, swapped b and h dimensions so h remains parallel to the buckling axis, Le populated with reported values, converted L/delta to raw w0 values in mm, Authors recorded an additional test setup eccentricity (e), meaning the physical columns experienced a total effective imperfection (e+δ) somewhat larger than the raw natural bow w0​ captured here.

study_id_notes: 6 distinct batches (buchanan2018_A through F) cleanly separated by cross-section size, grade and tube origin
judgement_calls: Mapped outer diameter D to b and left h null, prioritised compressive stub column material properties over tensile coupons for primary parameters, excluded all 10 stub columns, all 450 FEA models and 3 very short pin-ended ferritic columns that failed by premature local buckling

study_id_notes: 2 distinct physical material batches (ning2021_A and ning2021_B) corresponding to the 2 tested hot-rolled CHS nominal sizes (114×6 and 219×6), as each shares a unique set of averaged tensile coupon results. All 115 FEA models were excluded
judgement_calls: Assigned buckling_axis as - due to perfectly symmetric CHS profiles. Mapped outer diameter D to b and left h null to trigger downstream circular calculations. Extracted material properties from tensile flat coupons rather than compressive properties, as no compressive tests were performed. Excluded all 115 FEA models to prevent artificial dataset inflation and leakage across train/test splits. Stage 2 Flag: The authors determined the average applied loading eccentricity e, natural bow δ0, and total equivalent initial eccentricity eEq. Recorded δ0 as w0, e as we, and eEq as wtotal; the Stage 2 ML model must use this combined wtotal value.

study_id_notes: 2 batches --> rasmussen1993_A for SHS and rasmussen1993_B for CHS
judgement_calls: Prioritised compressive stub column material properties over individual tensile flat coupons to accurately capture the composite compressive stiffness. Assigned buckling_axis as - due to geometrically symmetric SHS and CHS profiles. Left h null for CHS. Computed effective length programmatically (Le​=L+450 mm) based on the test bearings adding 225 mm to each end. Excluded all 4 stub columns. Stage 2 Flag: Authors explicitly tested half the columns concentrically and half eccentrically. Mapped the measured natural bow v0​ to w0, the applied nominal eccentricity e0​ to we (acting against the natural bow), and the combined effective distance from the line of action

study_id_notes: 2 distinct physical material batches (yuan2015_A for Austenitic 1.4301 and yuan2015_B for Duplex 1.4462) separated by grade each sharing a unique set of Longitudinal Tension (LT) coupon results.
judgement_calls:
Extracted primary material properties only from Longitudinal Tension LT flat coupons. Set buckling_axis to minor. Removed all 342 parametric FEA models to avoid data leakage and avoid artificially increasing the dataset size.
Stage 2 Flag: Converted the paper’s global curvature v0 to w0 converted load eccentricity ec to w_e and converted equivalent initial eccentricity eEq to w_total while deliberately ignoring the paper’s local imperfection amplitude w0. Set failure_mode to interactive for all 10 extracted rows because local overall interactive buckling was the clear and observed failure mechanism.

study_id_notes: Three distinct physical material batches were used young2002_A for Series C1 young2002_B for Series C2 and young2002_C for Series C3. These were cleanly separated by cross section series and each batch shared a unique set of stub column material properties.
judgement_calls:
Prioritised compressive stub column parameters over tensile coupons to capture composite compressive stiffness accurately. Set buckling_axis to a dash because CHS profiles are fully symmetric. Computed the effective length automatically using Le = 0.5 × L based on fixed fixed boundary conditions. Removed all four stub columns. Failure modes for individual long columns were not listed explicitly by the authors and were only grouped loosely in the text so they were left for the inferred_failure_mode script to classify using Eurocode limits.
Stage 2 Flag: Converted the tabulated delta over L ratios to w0 by multiplying by L. Set w_e to zero because the bearings were locked for full contact with no applied eccentricity which made w_total equal to w0

study_id_notes: Two distinct physical material batches were used yang2016_A for Austenitic S304 and yang2016_B for Duplex S2205. These were cleanly separated by material grade and each batch shared a unique set of tensile coupon results.
judgement_calls:
Extracted material properties exclusively from flat tensile coupons as compressive tests were not conducted. Set failure_mode to global_flexural for all tests based on clear physical observation which directly overrode the theoretical Eurocode Class 4 classification for the slender sections. Mapped effective length Le to the authors L t column to account for hinge dimensions. Removed all FEA models to prevent data leakage.
Stage 2 Flag: Mapped w_0 to the maximum initial bow δ0. Calculated w_e programmatically as the average of the top and bottom loading eccentricities e0b plus e0t divided by two. Computed w_total as w_0 plus w_e.

study_id_notes: Two distinct physical material batches were used liu2003_A for Series S1 and liu2003_B for Series S2. These were separated perfectly by the two cross section sizes and each batch relied on its own unique set of averaged stub column material properties.
judgement_calls:
Extracted primary material properties strictly from the compressive stub column tests. Removed all four stub column tests. Computed the effective length as L multiplied by zero point five based on the explicit fixed fixed bearing setup. Set buckling_axis to a dash because the SHS profiles were perfectly symmetric. Left failure_mode as null because the authors described failure only loosely in text and not per specimen so it was flagged for the inferred_failure_mode script to classify using Eurocode limits.
Stage 2 Flag: Calculated w_0 by taking the maximum initial bow ratio delta_x over L or delta_y over L and multiplying by L. Set w_e to zero because the spherical bearings were fully locked concentrically with no applied eccentricity which made w_total equal to w_0.

study_id_notes: Four distinct physical material batches were used young2003_A through young2003_D and these were separated by the four cross section series R1 R2 R3 and R4. Each batch used its own unique set of averaged stub column material properties.
judgement_calls:
Extracted primary material properties strictly from compressive stub columns to capture the full section cold worked state. Removed all eight stub column tests with L equal to 360 mm to prevent pure local failure modes from entering the global flexural dataset. Computed the effective length as L multiplied by zero point five based on the explicit fixed fixed boundary setup. Set buckling_axis to minor because minor axis geometric imperfections were tracked and RHS sections naturally buckle about their weaker axis. Left failure_mode as null because the authors reported mixed failure modes broadly in text but did not label them per specimen so it was flagged for the inferred_failure_mode algorithm.
Stage 2 Flag: Calculated w_0 by extracting the delta over L ratio from Table 7 and multiplying by L. Set w_e to zero because the spherical end bearings were concentrically locked after a two kN seating load to prevent initial loading eccentricity which made w_total equal to w_0.

study_id_notes: Fifteen distinct physical material batches were used and these were separated logically by the fifteen distinct cross section geometries. Each unique tube geometry linked to its own specific set of averaged compressive flat CF coupon material properties derived in Part 1.
judgement_calls: Forming a complete dataset required bridging two companion papers. Extracted n E0 sigma_0 point 2 and sigma_1 point 0 exclusively from the compressive flat CF coupons in Part 1 to apply to the long columns in Part 2. Removed all thirty seven stub columns and nine beam tests to prevent local buckling and flexural bending contamination. Left failure_mode as null because the authors reported mixed failure modes broadly but did not assign them to individual specimens so it was flagged for the inferred_failure_mode algorithm.

study_id_notes: Four distinct physical material batches were used young2006_A through young2006_D and these were separated perfectly by the four tested cross section series SHS1 SHS2 RHS1 and RHS2.
judgement_calls:
Included interactive modes by keeping nine global flexural F failures and five interactive LCF failures while strictly excluding all six stub columns with Y over L and all four pure local L long columns. Used a hybrid material selection approach by extracting properties from compressive stub tests in Table 6 strictly for Series SHS1 but reverting to flat tensile coupons in Table 5 for SHS2 RHS1 and RHS2 because the authors stated clearly that stub column local buckling artificially suppressed their zero point two percent proof stresses. Computed Le as L multiplied by zero point five based on the rigid fixed fixed setup. Populated failure_mode directly from the authors explicit tags rather than using the inference script. Set buckling_axis to minor for RHS and set it to a dash for SHS.
Stage 2 Flag: Calculated w_0 by taking the maximum of the two orthogonal initial bow ratios delta_x over L or delta_y over L and multiplying by L. Set w_e to zero because the bearings were concentrically locked after a two kN seating load which meant w_total equalled w_0.

study_id_notes: 1 distinct material batch (sun2020_A). The authors explicitly stated that all coupons were cut from the exact same batch of virgin plates used to fabricate the 10 column specimens.
judgement_calls:
Material selection used material properties taken strictly from the virgin plate longitudinal tensile coupons which is the correct baseline for welded sections where thermal residual stresses dominate over cold working. All seventy FEA parametric study results were removed to prevent data leakage in the ML models and only the ten physical tests were kept. Set failure_mode to global_flexural because the authors stated clearly that all tested specimens failed by member flexural buckling about the minor axis.
Stage 2 Flag: Mapped w_0 directly to the measured initial geometric bow omega_g. Mapped w_total to the combined imperfection and initial loading eccentricity omega_g plus e0 reported by the authors. Computed w_e as the difference which was e0.

study_id_notes: 9 distinct material batches. Each unique cross-section geometry maps directly to its own specific material batch and corresponding tensile coupon properties in Table 3
judgement_calls:
All nine stub columns were removed because they failed by inelastic local buckling. For cross sections made from plates with different thicknesses the tensile properties were taken from the flange F coupons because the flanges control the flexural stability of the section. The forming route was categorised as laser_welded so that the uniquely low residual stress patterns from this fabrication method were captured by the ML models. Set failure_mode to global_flexural based on the authors clear confirmations.
Stage 2 Flag: Mapped w_0 to the measured initial global bow v0. Mapped w_total to the combined total eccentricity v0 plus e0 determined from strain gauges during the tests. Computed w_e mathematically as the setup eccentricity e0.
